blocks.content: After practice Jonathan and Marcus were still on the field. I walked out alone — through the gym hallway, past the weight room, out the side door.
There was a group of girls coming out of the gym. Four of them. Loud in that Friday-afternoon way where school is finally over and there's nowhere to be.
One of them I recognized. Stacy.
She was walking with her friends and she looked over at me and then past me back toward the field and I realized she wasn't looking at me at all.
She didn't find him in time — she kept walking with her group, said something that made one of her friends laugh — and then they were gone around the corner.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You were still talking to Marcus.
He'd asked about nothing. He didn't know what he'd walked back too late to see. And I wasn't going to tell him — not yet. But I filed it.
